title: botrychium alaskense
intro: Botrychium alaskense, also known as Alaska grape fern or Alaska adder's tongue fern, is a rare and endangered species of plant belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Botrychium alaskense are attributed to its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and grinding the roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
